authority: Founder directive 'START BUILDING Isolated (worktree) Autorun ON GHA CLOUDFLARE RAILWAY SUPABASE' 2026-07-09
supersedes_local_gap: The prior mcp scheduled-tasks routine 'category-registry-drift-guard' only runs while Claude Code is open on this Mac. This build adds a real cloud execution/verification/history/monitoring stack. The local routine is LEFT RUNNING as a fallback until Phase 2/3 below are proven — not disabled.

architecture.GHA: isolated ephemeral runner + explicit git worktree add, runs scripts/category_registry_drift_check_v1.py (read-only), workflow_dispatch only, schedule commented pending FOUNDER-UNLOCK-DRIFT-CRON
architecture.Cloudflare: independent verifier (workers/category-drift-verifier) — re-derives drift/missing counts from the receipt's own results array and rejects any mismatch; proves separate account+edge runtime via cf-ray + secondary account id
architecture.Supabase: durable queryable history (category_drift_status_v1 current-state + category_drift_runs_v1 run-history), same pattern as workflow_census_v1.sql, no FK back into product/
architecture.Railway: dead-man's-switch monitor — independently polls the Cloudflare Worker's /category-drift/latest on its own clock, alerts if stale beyond MAX_AGE_HOURS; the only layer that catches total pipeline silence

testing_performed_before_shipping: Ran scripts/category_registry_drift_check_v1.py against the REAL registry twice; caught and fixed 2 real bugs (bare-filename resolution, and a same-repo-different-branch false-negative that would have masked real drift) before ever wiring it to a cloud service

Confirmed both modes work: local full check (--workspace-root) and cloud-scoped check (no cross-repo access) correctly agree on the 2 genuinely missing proof assets and correctly separate 6 out-of-scope (cross-repo) findings from real drift
node --check on both the Cloudflare Worker and the Railway monitor: syntax OK

Extracted the Worker's verifyReceiptConsistency logic and ran it in plain Node against the REAL generated receipt: independently recomputed drifted=2, missing=2, out_of_scope=6 — exact agreement with the Python script's own count, proving the two independently-written layers actually agree

Started the Railway monitor for real (not just syntax-checked) and hit /health, /status, and a 404 path — all correct; separately pointed it at an unreachable URL and confirmed it reports ok:false without crashing (the actual failure-detection path was exercised, not just the happy path)
GHA workflow YAML parsed and confirmed the schedule trigger is absent (workflow_dispatch only) — the founder-gate is real, not just a comment
known_first_run_findings_not_fixed: CAT-03: sina-governance-SSOT/skills/p0pgr-skills-workspace does not exist on branch cursor/product-category-lock-v1 (exists on cursor/language-layer-v1 — likely worktree/branch divergence, not a real regression)
CAT-05: sina-governance-SSOT/skills/pr-conflict-resolver/PR_CONFLICT_RESOLVER_SKILL_LOCKED_v1.md similarly missing on this branch
Not fixed here deliberately: this is a read-only drift GUARD, not an editor; these are exactly the kind of findings it exists to surface for founder review, not to silently patch
phase_2_founder_actions_required_to_go_l: GitHub: add repo secrets CATEGORY_DRIFT_VERIFIER_URL, SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY
Cloudflare: wrangler kv:namespace create DRIFT_RECEIPTS, paste id into wrangler.category-drift-verifier.jsonc, wrangler deploy
Railway: new service from autorun/category-drift-guard/railway-monitor, set env vars from .env.example (real CATEGORY_DRIFT_VERIFIER_URL + optional ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL)
Manually trigger .github/workflows/category-drift-guard-v1.yml via workflow_dispatch to prove R1->R2 cloud-manual, exactly like the P0-PGR precedent
Only after a FOUNDER-UNLOCK-DRIFT-CRON receipt: uncomment the schedule: line in category-drift-guard-v1.yml
